name: Charlie Mariano
abstract: Carmine Ugo Mariano (November 12, 1923 – June 16, 2009) was an American jazz saxophonist who focused on the alto and soprano saxophone. He occasionally performed and recorded on flute and nadaswaram as well.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Mariano, who is one of my all-time favorite alto players, was in the band at that time. ... So I asked Charlie about this idea of writing a longer piece for the quintet, and he was all for it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: Mariano and I went to Europe for … we had a club engagement in Paris for four weeks, and then two weeks in each, one in Copenhagen, and another one in Sweden... Then we went back to Japan again... So we came back to the States at the end of ‟64.
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: I heard that Toshiko and Charlie Mariano, who was her husband, were looking for a drummer... I auditioned for her and I got the job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Holman
about_quotes.evidence: I did one on “Stella By Starlight” that featured Charlie Mariano on alto sax
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Holman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Tell me a little bit about the Schillinger House and Berklee, when you were over there with Herb Pomeroy and those guys. >> [INTERVIEWEE] And Charlie Mariano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: they had great soloists — Charlie Mariano, Lenny Niehaus, Frank Rosolino and Carl Fontana and there was a lot of good soloists on the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997
